decks.notes: The deck focuses on assembling a powerful combo using Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II to generate advantage and overwhelm the opponent. Thoughtseize and Fatal Push help control the opponent's board, while cards like Monument to Endurance and Guardian of New Benalia provide support and protection for the combo pieces. The deck aims to set up a strong board presence and disrupt the opponent's strategy while working towards its combo win condition.

This UW Control deck focuses on controlling the game through a combination of counter spells, card draw, and removal. Beza, the Bounding Spring and Emeritus of Ideation provide card advantage, while Teferi, Hero of Dominaria offers card draw and board control. March of Otherworldly Light and Ultima serve as win conditions. The deck also includes land destruction with cards like Deserted Beach and Restless Anchorage. Overall, the key strategy of this deck is to disrupt the opponent's game plan, control the board, and eventually win through card advantage and powerful late-game threats.

This Pioneer 1 vs 1 deck focuses on assembling a powerful combo involving Greasefang and Parhelion II. The deck includes various support cards to help draw into the combo pieces, protect them, and generate value. With a mix of creatures, spells, and lands to facilitate the combo, the goal is to overwhelm the opponent with a powerful board presence once the combo is assembled. Additionally, the deck includes disruption and removal elements to control the game and ensure the combo can be successfully executed.

This deck focuses on using a combination of hand disruption, removal spells, and powerful creatures to control the game and outvalue the opponent. It aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan with cards like Duress and Thoughtseize, while removing threats with Fatal Push and Abrupt Decay. The deck also utilizes powerful creatures like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse and Professor Dellian Fel to pressure the opponent and close out the game. By controlling the board and disrupting the opponent's strategy, this deck seeks to outmaneuver and outlast the opposition.

This deck focuses on quickly casting Arclight Phoenix by playing cheap spells, then using spells like Artist's Talent and Fiery Impulse to control the board while dealing damage to the opponent. It also utilizes Treasure Cruise and Picklock Prankster to maintain card advantage and fuel more spells to bring back Arclight Phoenix. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of aggression and card advantage, making it difficult for them to keep up and ultimately leading to victory through a fast and powerful strategy.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly ramp up mana through cards like Lotus Field and Arboreal Grazer, allowing the player to cast powerful spells like Scapeshift and Terror of the Peaks. The deck also utilizes Aftermath Analyst and Lumra, Bellow of the Woods for card advantage and board control. The overall strategy of the deck is to create a fast and efficient combo that overwhelms the opponent with powerful creatures and massive damage output.

This Red Deck Wins Pioneer deck focuses on aggressive creature and burn spells to quickly deal damage to the opponent and control the board. With a mix of efficient creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Emberheart Challenger, along with versatile burn spells like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage, this deck aims to keep the pressure on the opponent and close out games quickly. Mutavault and Den of the Bugbear provide additional threats, while Kumano, Master Yamabushi acts as a powerful finisher. Overall, this deck's strategy is to apply constant pressure and burn out the opponent before they can stabilize.

The deck focuses on aggressive plays using a combination of red and blue spells to control the board and deal damage quickly. With a mix of burn spells, card draw, and creatures with evasion, the deck aims to apply early pressure and maintain tempo throughout the game. The inclusion of Ral, Crackling Wit and Stormchaser's Talent highlights the focus on spellcasting synergy, while cards like Divide by Zero provide disruptive elements. By utilizing a mix of direct damage, counterspells, and efficient creatures, this deck aims to quickly overwhelm opponents and secure a victory.

This deck focuses on controlling the game by using a combination of counterspells, card draw, and removal spells to disrupt the opponent's strategy. It aims to maintain board presence through efficient removal spells and card advantage, while also having access to powerful finishers like Teferi, Hero of Dominaria and Ultima. The deck uses a mix of creatures, enchantments, and planeswalkers to both control the board and pressure the opponent, ultimately aiming to outvalue and outlast the opponent in a grindy game.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly build up a strong board presence with aggressive green creatures and use them to generate massive amounts of green mana through devotion. This mana can then be used to play powerful spell cards like Nature's Rhythm and The Great Henge to further strengthen the board and overwhelm the opponent. The deck also includes a few large creatures like Emrakul, the Promised End and Craterhoof Behemoth to finish off the opponent quickly once the mana is in place.

This Dimir Aggro deck focuses on aggressive creature plays to quickly overwhelm the opponent while also utilizing disruptive spells like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push to control the board and maintain tempo. The deck also includes powerful finishers like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse and Kaito, Bane of Nightmares to close out games. Multiversal Passage and Moon-Circuit Hacker provide additional utility and flexibility in the deck's strategy. Overall, the deck aims to apply consistent pressure on the opponent while disrupting their game plan, leading to a fast and decisive victory.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly build up a strong board presence through early mana ramp and efficient creature cards, while utilizing green devotion to power up key cards such as The Great Henge and Nykthos, Shrine to Nyx. The deck focuses on aggression and overwhelming the opponent with powerful creatures like Craterhoof Behemoth and Emrakul, the Promised End, supported by utility spells like Fight Rigging and Nature's Rhythm. Additionally, the deck includes cards like Titan of Industry and Invasion of Ikoria to provide versatility and additional value in different game situations.

The main concept of this deck is to overwhelm opponents with aggressive red creatures and burn spells, focusing on dealing damage quickly and efficiently. This deck aims to control the board with efficient removal spells like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage while utilizing creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Emberheart Challenger to apply early pressure. The inclusion of cards like Kumano Faces Kakkazan and Screaming Nemesis allows for additional reach and finishing power, making it difficult for opponents to stabilize. Overall, this deck prioritizes speed and aggression to close out games as quickly as possible.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly play aggressive creatures and disrupt the opponent's strategy with hand disruption and removal spells. By combining efficient creatures like Kaito, Bane of Nightmares and Moon-Circuit Hacker with cards like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push, the deck aims to put pressure on the opponent while controlling the game. With a mix of evasive creatures and powerful finishers like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse, the deck looks to close out games quickly before the opponent can mount a defense. Additionally, the mana base is built to support the deck's color requirements and ensure consistency in casting spells.

The deck focuses on using removal spells and hand disruption to control the opponent's board and resources, while gradually building up its own board presence with efficient creatures like Badgermole Cub and Graveyard Trespasser. The inclusion of powerful threats like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse and Emeritus of Abundance adds additional late-game power. The deck also includes versatile lands like Mutavault and Urborg, Tomb of Yawgmoth to help fix mana and provide additional utility. Overall, the deck aims to outvalue and outgrind the opponent through careful resource management and timely removal.

The main concept of this deck is to utilize the Lotus Field card along with other ramp spells and lands to generate a large amount of mana quickly. This allows the player to cast powerful spells like Scapeshift to search for multiple lands and potentially win the game in one turn. The deck also includes cards to protect and support the combo pieces, such as Aftermath Analyst for card draw and Lumra, Bellow of the Woods for additional mana production. Overall, the deck aims to assemble its combo pieces efficiently and overwhelm the opponent with its powerful spells.
